title: Fraud Loss Reporting Aggregation by Product Line

compliance.assurance_rationale: Uplifted from contributing to partial: UC provides meaningful monitoring evidence for Art.96.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that PSD2 Art.96 (Incident reporting) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.22.29: Fraud Loss Reporting Aggregation by Product Line.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.22.29' running on index=payments pay:fraud:loss — incident_id, milestone, severity, impacted_psu_estimate, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

spl:
index=payments sourcetype="pay:fraud:loss" earliest=-400d
| eval inc_tag="loss"
| stats sum(loss_eur) as total_loss, dc(case_id) as cases by product_line, reporting_quarter
| eventstats median(total_loss) as med_loss by reporting_quarter
| eval uc_id="22.22.29"
| eval uplift=round(total_loss-med_loss,2)
| where uplift>10000 OR total_loss>250000
| sort - total_loss
| table uc_id, reporting_quarter, product_line, cases, total_loss, med_loss, uplift
description: Supports incident governance, customer communications evidence, and supervisory reporting workflows associated with payment service continuity and fraud statistics.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for PSD2 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes payments and sourcetypes pay:fraud:loss. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Normalize field extractions and CIM tags; (2) schedule hourly or near-real-time; (3) route alerts to SOC/compliance queues; (4) export evidence packs for supervisory review. Indexes required: `index=payments`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=pay:fraud:loss`.
visualization: Timeline (milestones), Table (late NCA notifications), Single value (mismatches). Key fields: `cases`, `inc_tag`, `med_loss`, `product_line`, `reporting_quarter`, `total_loss`.
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
grandmaExplanation: We watch for supports incident governance, customer communications evidence, and supervisory reporting workflows associated with payment service continuity and fraud statistics. This matters because it catches problems that could lead to audit findings or compliance gaps.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk ITSI (1841).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=payments` `pay:fraud:loss` — incident_id, milestone, severity, impacted_psu_estimate.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Normalize field extractions and CIM tags; (2) schedule hourly or near-real-time; (3) route alerts to SOC/compliance queues; (4) export evidence packs for supervisory review.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=payments sourcetype="pay:fraud:loss" earliest=-400d
| eval inc_tag="loss"
| stats sum(loss_eur) as total_loss, dc(case_id) as cases by product_line, reporting_quarter
| eventstats median(total_loss) as med_loss by reporting_quarter
| eval uc_id="22.22.29"
| eval uplift=round(total_loss-med_loss,2)
| where uplift>10000 OR total_loss>250000
| sort - total_loss
| table uc_id, reporting_quarter, product_line, cases, total_loss, med_loss, uplift
```

Understanding this SPL

**Fraud Loss Reporting Aggregation by Product Line (PSD2 / EBA fraud reporting)** — Supports incident governance, customer communications evidence, and supervisory reporting workflows associated with payment service continuity and fraud statistics.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=payments` `pay:fraud:loss` — incident_id, milestone, severity, impacted_psu_estimate. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk ITSI (1841). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: payments; **sourcetype**: pay:fraud:loss. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=payments, sourcetype="pay:fraud:loss", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **inc_tag** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `stats` aggregates events by **product_line, reporting_quarter**, producing one row per unique combination for trend analysis and threshold evaluation.
• `eventstats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by reporting_quarter** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
• `eval` defines or adjusts **uc_id** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **uplift** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Filters the current rows with `where uplift>10000 OR total_loss>250000` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.

Output fields: cases, inc_tag, med_loss, product_line, reporting_quarter, total_loss, uc_id, uplift.

Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Timeline (milestones), Table (late NCA notifications), Single value (mismatches)
